body_en: Use this page first for daily health habits, diet basics, blood pressure and sugar monitoring, and simple safety notes.
Eat regular meals, drink water, move daily, sleep consistently, take medicines as prescribed, and record readings if advised.
Half vegetables, quarter protein, quarter starch. Water is the default drink.
Patients on insulin or medicines that can cause low sugar should not skip meals without a care plan.
One reading is not the whole story. Track patterns and symptoms.
Very high blood pressure with chest pain, severe shortness of breath, weakness, speech difficulty, or vision change needs emergency care.
Supportive care is often enough. Watch for breathing difficulty, chest pain, confusion, dehydration, or worsening illness.
Most cases are viral. Rest, fluids, and symptom care are usually enough.
Follow your care plan, track sugar as advised, and know the symptoms of low and high sugar.
Seek emergency care now for chest pain, pressure, or heaviness, especially with sweating, shortness of breath, fainting, or weakness.
Antibiotics do not treat most viral colds. Use only when prescribed.
Medication and insulin plans during fasting need doctor review before Ramadan.
